checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?
Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?
Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities=67198410.26 (FY2013) vs NetIncomeLoss=21129265.21 (FY2013) - ratio ~3.2x, compared to FY2015 ratio of only ~1.2x, showing a deteriorating OCF/NI relationship

checklist.question: Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: CostOfGoodsAndServicesSold last reported value=2741131.57 (FY2011, 2010-07-01 to 2011-06-30); no further CostOfGoodsAndServicesSold data points provided after 2012-05-10 filing

InventoryNet=1439152.20 (2015-06-30) remains a very small balance relative to Revenues=698074839.24 (FY2015), consistent with a service/capital-intensive business model where inventory is not a primary driver

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss quarterly progression: 6237101.18 (Q1FY15), 6620720.10 (Q2FY15), 6658694.50 (Q3FY15), 9326977.22 (Q4FY15), 8809285.42 (Q1FY16), 5126543.77 (Q2FY16), 4060160.67 (Q3FY16) - relatively smooth compared to NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities which swung from 34537327.77 (FY2015 annual) to -17675144.94 (Q1FY16 quarterly)

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=809862.17 (2011-06-30) against AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=50159530.24 (2011-06-30), a coverage ratio of ~1.61%

AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=776537.70 (2015-06-30) against AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=101439692.72 (2015-06-30), a coverage ratio of only ~0.77%, despite AR having roughly doubled since 2011

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: 10-K/A filed 2012-05-18, followed by 10-Q/A filings on 2012-05-25 for both the 2011-09-30 and 2011-12-31 quarters
10-K/A filed 2015-03-30, roughly six months after the original 10-K filed 2014-09-15

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Financial series data span from CashAndCashEquivalentsAtCarryingValue=668814.40 (2009-06-30) through AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=101661339.21 (2016-03-31), covering ~7 years of quarterly and annual data across ~20 distinct XBRL concepts

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Bad debt reserve was not increased in proportion to the growth and aging of receivables (DSO rose from ~38 days in FY2011-12 to ~48 days by FY2014), causing allowance coverage to fall from ~1.61% of AR (2011-06-30) to ~0.77% of AR (2015-06-30). An under-reserved allowance understates bad debt expense, overstating net receivables and net income.

Revenue and receivables grew faster than cash collections, consistent with revenue being recognized ahead of cash receipt (e.g., aggressive percentage-of-completion or bill-and-hold practices). This produced a widening income-statement/cash-flow gap culminating in a negative operating cash flow quarter despite continued net income growth.

Multiple post-issuance amendments (10-K/A and 10-Q/A) in 2012 covering the FY2011 10-K and both the September and December 2011 quarters, plus a further 10-K/A in March 2015 for the FY2014 10-K, indicate the company identified and corrected errors in previously filed financial statements, raising the possibility that undetected or partially corrected misstatements remain in the underlying balances (receivables, PP&E, or liabilities) carried into subsequent periods.

rationale_evidence.quote: 10-K/A filed 2012-05-18 and 10-Q/A filings filed 2012-05-25 for periods ended 2011-09-30 and 2011-12-31

top_signals: Allowance for doubtful accounts coverage ratio fell from ~1.61% of AR (2011-06-30) to ~0.77% of AR (2015-06-30) even as AR nearly doubled and DSO lengthened from ~38 to ~48 days

Operating cash flow diverged sharply from net income, turning negative (-17.7M in Q1 FY2016) despite continued positive/growing net income (8.8M same quarter), and the FY2015 OCF/NI ratio (~1.2x) fell well below the FY2013 ratio (~3.2x)
History of financial statement amendments: 10-K/A and dual 10-Q/A filings in 2012 covering FY2011 and two subsequent quarters, plus another 10-K/A in March 2015 for the FY2014 10-K
Rapid receivables growth outpacing revenue growth in FY2013-FY2015 alongside a debt-funded, capital-intensive expansion (goodwill nearly quadrupled, large debt issuances)
